capability_objective: The events lead is checking lodging holds referenced in two venue notes. A previous note omitted stable IDs, so every conclusion must trace to a retrieved record. Find the reservation named “Foundry Hall Reception” at “Pittsburgh” and the reservation named “Glasshouse Dinner Reservation” at “Cleveland”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Write one compact sentence per record in the requested order, followed by a third sentence that compares only their statuses.
prompt:
The events lead needs a checked lodging-hold note for two venue records. Work only in the supplied sandbox and create `lodging-audit.txt` from full reservation records, not search summaries or backing files. Read `LODGING.md`, then use Pi's shell tool to run the genuine local `./lodgectl` executable for every lodging-registry lookup; use its built-in help to discover the interface. Do not inspect or edit the executable implementation, protected seed, runtime database, execution journal, protected setup, or verifier. Do not write a script, wrapper, function, or loop to perform the lookup workflow.

Find the reservation named “Foundry Hall Reception” at “Pittsburgh” and the reservation named “Glasshouse Dinner Reservation” at “Cleveland”. Your first lodging-data assistant action must start both exact name-and-location searches concurrently as two separate direct `./lodgectl` processes, with no other lodging operation in that action, and wait for both results. Do not begin any retrieval until both searches have returned. Resolve each branch independently: retrieve it only if its own search returned exactly one match containing one nonempty stable ID, using only that returned ID. If both branches resolve, your immediately following lodging-data assistant action must start both full-record gets concurrently as two separate direct `./lodgectl` processes and wait for both. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, do not guess or retrieve that branch; leave it unresolved while completing any independently resolved branch. Take every reported name, location, stable ID, and status for a resolved branch only from its successful full-record get.

This is a read-only audit. Do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. Do not alter lodging data, inspect or edit the execution journal, expose unrelated records, or include other full-record fields. Do not invoke any lodging operation beyond the two required searches and eligible full-record gets.

`lodging-audit.txt` must contain exactly three nonblank, newline-terminated lines with no heading, bullets, blank lines, or other prose. The first two lines must each be exactly one sentence in the requested reservation order. Format a resolved line exactly as `<name> at <location> [<stable ID>] has retrieved status <status>.` Format an unresolved line exactly as `<requested name> at <requested location> [UNRESOLVED] is unresolved.` The third line must be exactly `Status comparison: both are <status>.` when both retrieved statuses are identical, `Status comparison: <first status> differs from <second status>.` when both are present and differ, or `Status comparison: unknown.` when either branch is unresolved. Substitute only grounded values, preserve retrieved spelling and punctuation, and include no placeholders or angle brackets. After writing the file, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`. Leave all supplied files unchanged and make the final assistant response exactly the file's content.
